steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a historical similarity between Hadid’s The Peak and Gehry’s Gehry House. “Historical similarity” means showing the two projects share the same place in history — not just that they look alike.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The popularization of deconstructivist design in the late twentieth century owes much to the architects featured in MoMA's 1988 exhibition, Hadid and Gehry among them.’: credits many architects for popularizing deconstructivism; the two projects are mentioned only in passing and no clear equivalence is drawn. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Hadid's and Gehry's respective projects date to 1978, a decade before MoMA's influential 1988 exhibition Deconstructivist Architecture.’: states an incorrect date — The Peak is from 1983, not 1978. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘With their jagged geometric forms, The Peak and Gehry House both subvert conventional assumptions "about order, harmony, stability, and unity" (Johnson and Wigley).’: describes a design similarity (shared aesthetic), not a historical one. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The Peak and Gehry House were featured in an influential 1988 MoMA exhibition that popularized deconstructivist design principles in the late twentieth century.’: places both projects in the same defining historical moment — the 1988 MoMA exhibition that popularized deconstructivist design. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
